title: Sharah-i-Qanun-i-Miad-i-Sama'at, Hind, Act 9, 1908
gloss: Translation of Act 9 of 1908 on Limitation, annotated rendered into Urdu by Shaikh Ghulam Rasul.
notes: 862-pp. annotated Urdu translation of the Indian Limitation Act 1908 — the colonial legal code vernacularized for native practitioners; the law-in-translation genre (self-representation of the imperial statute in the vernacular market).

title: Sharah-i-Qanun-i-Registry Dastawezat, Act 16, 1908
gloss: Or Act No. 16 of 1908, annotated, translated by Shaikh Ghulam Rasul.
notes: Urdu annotation of the Registration Act 1908 — Ghulam Rasul's colonial-law-translation series continues (s1-2).

author: Ghulam Ahmad, M.
title: Mujarrabat-i-Qadiri
gloss: A book which contains short descriptions of important diseases and gives prescriptions for their treatment.
notes: Yunani medical formulary, 5000 copies — a very large medical print run; the Ghulam Ahmad medical publishing operation of Ludhiana (s1-3, shared copyright note).

author: Ghulam Ahmad, M.
title: Purana Kashmiri Kok Shastar
gloss: A book describing the different temperaments of men and women and particularly dealing with venereal diseases.
copyright: M. Ghulam Ahmad, Bookseller, Ludhiana; No. 30, dated the 28th February, 1910.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s1. 'Old Kashmiri Kok Shastra' — an erotology/sexology text (Koka Shastra tradition) reframed as venereal-disease medicine; the sexual-health quackery market. Copyright note spans s2-3.

author: Ghulam Ahmad, M.
title: Raz-i-Tib
gloss: A book which treats of diseases and gives prescriptions for their treatment.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s2 (Ghulam Ahmad). Third of his 1910Q1 medical titles.

title: Hamdard-i-Nau Jawanan
gloss: A pamphlet dealing with venereal diseases of young men addicted to bad habits and containing suggestions for their treatment.
notes: 'Friend of Youth' venereal-disease/masturbation-anxiety tract, 4000 copies at 1 anna — mass-market moral-medical panic literature; publisher 'The Sanyasi Ashram, Sargodha' (a Hindu-ascetic imprint on Muslim-associated sexual-health themes). Bracket note: previous edition at entry No. 17, page 17 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending 30th September 1909.

author: Joshi, M. S.
title: Khulasat-ul-Hikmat
gloss: A treatise containing certain instructions for the preservation of health.

author: Karim Bakhsh
title: Ilaj-i-Daktri
gloss: A booklet describing the treatment of some important diseases according to the western system of medicine, for the use of Native Hakims and other people.
notes: 'Doctor's Treatment' — Western allopathic medicine explicitly packaged 'for the use of Native Hakims': the hybridization of Yunani and Western medicine, a direct instance of colonial knowledge being absorbed into the indigenous practitioner's repertoire (the reification/appropriation dynamic in the medical field).
